title: Squash and fixup commit semantics
description: Squash combines commits and lets you edit the joined message, while fixup folds a commit into the previous one and discards its message automatically.
body: Use when collapsing review-iteration commits during an interactive rebase. Do not lose meaningful messages by using fixup when the wording matters. Squash merges a commit into its predecessor and opens an editor to combine messages; fixup does the same but silently drops the squashed commit's message, ideal for amend-style follow-ups created with commit --fixup. Inspect the rebase todo list to choose per commit. Validation signals include a final history whose messages describe the change accurately. Risk boundary: fixup permanently drops text, so review the resulting messages before force-pushing.
